source_notes.exports: Kpler public data — Novorossiysk weekly crude+product volumes

strike_weights.notes: Weighted score capped at 100. Rolling 30-day window.

export_baseline.description: Pre-war annual average Novorossiysk crude+product export volume used as 100% baseline for inversion score.

sources: Kyiv Post, June 11 2026 — R-280 corridor traffic down 71% over two weeks (Brovdi). Primary GLOC linking Russia to Crimea via Mariupol/Berdiansk/Melitopol.
Kyiv Independent, June 10 2026 — Mariupol port disabled: substations, radar, fuel storage, and control tower struck by Ukrainian forces (Azov Corps). Port assessed no longer operational.
ISW: cascading GLOC effects on Crimea resupply likely.
RFE/RL June 18 2026 — Moscow Kapotnya oil refinery struck for second time in one week; fuel rationing confirmed in 53 Russian regions.
Energy Intelligence via Inkorr June 18 2026 — 16 refinery facilities hit total, including 8 of Russia's 10 largest; ~2.14 million barrels/day offline; crude processing at 21-year low.
monthly_readings.strike_score_methodology: Mariupol port strike June 10: export_terminal (3pts) + storage (2pts) + control infrastructure (3pts structural-outage classification) = 8pts. Moscow Kapotnya June 12 + June 18 (two hits): refinery (3pts × 2) = 6pts additional. 16 facilities total including 8 of Russia's 10 largest — cumulative series now at 95/100, reflecting ~2.14 million bbl/day offline.
monthly_readings.export_pressure_methodology: R-280 corridor -71% in two weeks. Inverted: 100 - (29% remaining traffic as % of baseline) = 71, then adjusted to 77 for partial-month effect and Mariupol port compound loss. Held at 77 pending updated corridor report.
monthly_readings.note: STRUCTURAL EVENT: Mariupol port disabled + R-280 corridor -71% + Moscow Kapotnya struck twice in one week. RPI updated to 88 (lo:80/hi:96) reflecting escalation of national fuel crisis to 53 regions. CREA capacity data pending.

alert_thresholds.notes: Above 50 = meaningful fiscal pressure on occupation funding. Above 70 = acute threat to SCI sustainability.
